page: Amazon Web Services t3a.medium is a burstable general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 8 MB L3 cache. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks position the t3a.medium in the lower tiers of cloud compute resources. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, along with Geekbench scores, place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is measured at 77.52 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 20.08 GB/sec read and 10.67 GB/sec write for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic performance is slightly stronger, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the bottom 25% due to hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI support. Redis SET operations achieve 1.26 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.53 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3a.medium offers a highly economical entry point for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. Because it uses a burstable CPU allocation model, it is designed to accumulate CPU credits during idle periods to handle occasional spikes in demand. This resource profile is best suited for low-traffic web servers, small development or testing environments, microservices, and basic background tasks that can tolerate variable compute performance and do not require local storage or high baseline network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.medium is a burstable, budget-friendly instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results consistently place this instance in the bottom 10% for compute and memory bandwidth, though hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instructions provide moderate cryptographic capabilities. Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, the t3a.medium is highly cost-efficient for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, development environments, and lightweight background processes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.medium is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring a 2.2 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, optimized for low-cost workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.medium is a burstable x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, ideal for low-cost, light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable x86_64 instance with AMD EPYC processing and DDR4 memory for light, variable compute workloads.

Burstable CPU allocation model utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI instruction sets
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and development environments
